_doc: Periodic puller registry. Edit and SIGHUP the scheduler to reload (kill -HUP $(cat ~/Documents/scheduler.pid)). Per-job stdout/stderr → ~/Documents/scheduler_logs/<name>.log. Jobs with an `engine` field are auto-skipped when that engine is set to active=false in shadow_pnl/engines.json (single source of truth, session 7 v6).

jobs.name: t6_mlb_lines_puller
jobs._notes: T6 sharp Vegas line pull. 90-min cadence. Bankroll $18K → Kelly cap 5% = max $900 single. Auto-disabled if T6 flips to active=false.

jobs.name: entropy_collapse_detector
jobs._notes: Cross-engine informed-flow detector. No `engine` field — runs regardless of any single engine's state. After session 7 archive sweep, the only consumers left are T6/T7; if it generates noise for archived engines, scope it down then.

jobs.name: daily_status_report
jobs._notes: Daily status email. Fires every 5 min but the script no-ops unless current PT time is in [08:00, 08:10) AND today's date is not already in the marker file (~/Documents/terminal6_data/daily_report_last_sent.txt). One email per morning. Requires ~/Documents/gmail_smtp.env (gitignored via *.env rule).

jobs.name: daemon_watchdog
jobs._notes: Auto-restart silent engine daemons. For each active engine in engines.json with both monitoring.ws_logger_path and monitoring.redeploy_script, checks ws_logger.log mtime vs per-engine threshold. Stale → bash the redeploy_script. Backoff: 3 restarts in 1h → stop restarting that engine and email an ALERT (throttled 1/h) until human runs `python3 daemon_watchdog.py --reset-state`. State at scheduler_logs/daemon_watchdog_state.json. Audit at scheduler_logs/daemon_watchdog_restarts.log.

jobs.name: portfolio_freshness_watchdog
jobs._notes: Passive freshness detector. For each active engine in engines.json, checks monitoring.checks[] files vs max_age_h thresholds. Raises ~/Documents/freshness_alarm.flag when stale — that flag forces paper_trader into dry-run on next cycle. Complementary to daemon_watchdog (active auto-restart): freshness_watchdog *protects* via dry-run, daemon_watchdog *recovers* via restart. Both run at 5-min cadence.
